preFix.title: Button tufting a headboard
preFix.subtitle: Create a diamond-tufted upholstered headboard from a plywood base
preFix.excerpt: How to button-tuft a padded headboard: marking the diamond grid on foam and fabric, drilling through the base board, threading the button cord and pulling each button into the foam, and stapling the fabric cover to the back of the board.
preFix.sourceNotes: Practical Upholstery (C.H. Doust). Collins Complete DIY Manual. Reader's Digest Complete DIY Manual.

content.text: A button-tufted headboard is built on a plywood base, padded with foam and Dacron, then covered with fabric. The

pattern is drawn on the foam before cutting: the grid positions the buttons and determines the fold direction of the fabric between them. Mark the grid accurately because any error is multiplied across the finished surface.

A 12 mm or 18 mm plywood base cut to the headboard shape. High-density upholstery foam, 75 mm thick. Dacron wrap to layer over the foam. Fabric with at least 30 percent extra in each direction over the foam area to allow for tuck-in at the folds. Self-cover buttons on a shank (allow one per tuft position plus 10 per cent spares). Waxed
and a long upholstery needle. Staple gun and 14 mm staples. Cordless drill with a 6 mm bit.
Step 1, mark the grid and drill the base

Draw the diamond grid on the foam face: place buttons at the grid intersections, with rows offset by half a unit. Standard spacing is 100 to 150 mm between buttons. Transfer the grid onto the plywood base by pushing a skewer through the foam at each button point and marking the plywood below. Drill a 6 mm hole through the plywood at each mark.
Step 2, cover the foam and place the fabric

Fix the foam to the plywood with spray adhesive, then wrap the Dacron over the foam face and tack it lightly to the board edges. Lay the fabric face up over the Dacron; at each button position, push the tip of the upholstery regulator through the fabric and foam to locate the drill hole below.
Step 3, thread and pull the buttons

Thread button cord through the needle eye, then through the button shank. Push the needle through fabric and foam at the button position and out through the plywood hole. Thread the cord through a back-button or card square on the board back and tie off, pulling until the button sits at the right depth. Work from the grid centre outward; ease the fabric into neat diagonal folds with the regulator before tying off each button.

Once all buttons are tied, pull the fabric over the back of the board, folding the corners as you would wrap a parcel, and staple at 50 mm intervals. The fabric should be taut but not stretched so tight that it distorts the diamond folds on the face.
